data.description: Fix-it minted mid-MA-24 (blocks the re-record, which blocks MA-22). Live failure: gemini-3.1-pro-preview emits unified diffs whose hunk headers declare the full context-window counts (@@ -1,6 +1,5 @@) while the body truncates trailing context (actual 4 old/3 new) — patch 0.7 panics ('bug: failed to parse entire input. Remaining: use std::sync::Arc;'), caught by the existing catch_unwind and surfaced as applyEdit failure; retries exhaust; recording cannot complete. Exact failing input preserved in cassette fixtures/cassettes/896f6e7b4af1a7c7.json. Fix in falcon-mcp/src/tools/fs_basic.rs at the MA-16 seam: add a deterministic normalize step before parse_hunks that recounts each hunk's old/new line counts from the actual body lines (context/-/+) and rewrites the @@ header; tolerate missing trailing newline. Keep validate->normalize->parse->splice unit structure, keep the catch_unwind backstop, unit tests including the recorded failing diff verbatim as a regression fixture. Rust only; falcon-agent untouched (G-1); the planted-lint marker text appearing inside diff CONTENT in tests is data, not source.
data.title: Normalize model-emitted hunk headers before patch parse

data.body:
REVIEW (own-reviewer protocol) — diff d3f6c89 vs origin/main@9390f2c

VERDICT: APPROVE. No Critical or Major findings; one informational behavior note (accepted). Scope: exactly one file, falcon-mcp/src/tools/fs_basic.rs (+410/-5). falcon-agent untouched — G-1 verified by diff stat and check-planted-defects.sh exit 0.

Parser-adjacent hard checks (off-by-ones):
1. Hunks ending exactly at declared counts: recount == declared -> counts_differ false -> byte-identical passthrough, omitted-count style (@@ -1 +1 @@) preserved. Covered by normalize_passes_through_correct_counts_byte_identical / normalize_interprets_omitted_counts_as_one.
2. Adjacent hunks: next "@@" header is not a body prefix, so the body loop terminates exactly at the boundary; truncated hunk 1 + immediately-following correct hunk 2 covered by normalize_recounts_truncated_hunk_adjacent_to_correct_hunk (only hunk 1 rewritten, splice applies).
3. Empty context: a bare "" line is not a valid patch-0.7 chunk_line (context requires the ' ' prefix); classify_body_line returns None, mirroring the parser byte-for-byte — body ends there, nothing beyond it is counted or touched.
4. File-header exclusions match patch-0.7 exactly: "--- "/"+++ " excluded via the same semantics as not(tag("-- "))/not(tag("++ ")) after the sign char, including "---x" (counts as removal) and "-- x" (counts as removal) corners.
5. Shrink-only + nonzero-body guards: huge-count attack (@@ -1,4294967295 +1,1 @@, body 1 old/2 new) passes through untouched and still conflicts in splice_lines' checked bounds — unit mirror normalize_leaves_grown_body_counts_untouched plus the original integration regression both green. Body-less headers never rewritten to zero-count no-op hunks.
6. Recount overflow impossible: u64 counters, input byte-capped at 8 MiB upstream by validate_limits.
7. CRLF: terminator split/reattach preserves \r\n verbatim on rewrite.
8. Regression fixture byte-verified against the cassette: cmp of jq -j '.diff' (258 bytes, em-dash U+2014 intact) vs the test const — identical. Root cause confirmed from patch-0.7.0 source: parser is count-blind (many1(chunk_line)) and the panic is triggered by the unterminated final body line; both repairs (shrink-only recount + final-newline termination) are therefore necessary, and the catch_unwind backstop is unchanged.

Informational (accepted): a diff whose body GREW past declared counts and also lacks a final newline now gets the newline repair (header untouched) and can apply where it previously conflicted via parser panic. splice_lines still validates every context/remove line against the file, and unconditional newline repair is within the ticket's "handle missing trailing newline" mandate.

Gates: cargo fmt --check clean; cargo clippy -p falcon-mcp --all-targets -- -D warnings clean (CI's exact invocation); cargo test -p falcon-mcp 113/113 (66 lib incl. 10 new normalize tests + 47 integration); scripts/check-planted-defects.sh exit 0; falcon-detective npm test 146/146 (no TS changes, sanity).

PR opened: https://github.com/mrojas54/maltese-agent/pull/61 (head d3f6c891f0c39a9f9ab0809d742eded670db9142, base main). All gates green; review verdict APPROVE attached above. Ready for orchestrator merge.

Merged (PR #61, operator-authorized). Root cause: patch-0.7 parser is count-blind; missing trailing newline was the panic trigger, inflated counts separately broke splice bounds. Fixed both via shrink-only normalize_hunk_counts + newline termination at the MA-16 seam; operator's failing diff is the verbatim regression fixture. Unblocks MA-24 attempt #3.
